Step 5: The Grand Offer, Part 6
ALL CONTENT © COPYRIGHT 2011 AND BEYOND | JON BENSON 3X
FORMULAS, INC. | ALL RIGHTS RESERVED | NO DUPLICATION ALLOWED
Jon: 	Let’s get to part 6, the Q&A. This is where the 5-Step video sales letter
process ends. This is where your video sales letter ends. And it
includes the Q&A, another remember overview (because at this point,
you have nothing to lose), another call to action, testimonial slides.
And here, you can have 50, just don’t have any audio. Just have them
go by fast. You can have nothing but quotes, if you want. And then, a
final 3X CTA, with no audio, with an arrow pointing down. And this is
the end of the process.
So, here’s what that looks like. Let me tell you, first of all, what that
section is all about.
This is going to close the hangers-on, the people who are still not
convinced to buy. They’re sitting there, staring at their computer going,
“Man, I don’t know. Man, I don’t know.” This is who you’re targeting.
A lot of sales do come from the Q&A section, by the way. So, Q&A is
something most people leave out, so we don’t. And in this section, you
can go as long as you want. I can go about 3, 4, 5 minutes, maybe,
but you could go 30 minutes. Because, again, at this point, you have
nothing to lose. You have nothing to lose. No one is going to say,
“Oh, it’s just too long,” because they’ve already got the order button.
You could go for 3 days. It doesn’t really matter.
So, here are some examples slides of what I would do. Now, this is
after a 5-second pause. So, I would say, “You still there? If you’re still
watching, I bet you have some questions. And that’s okay. Here are
the most common questions I get, and the answers, to help you out.”
This is how I walk right into the Q&A section.

-- 1 of 3 --

2
And I do it very simply, like this. I say, “‘I lost interest faster than I lost
weight.’ Well, that’s probably because you weren’t given enough
variety. We all need variety, and that includes food we love to eat.”
And I would go on and read the Q&A. This is how I do my Q&A. You
could do it any way you wanted to, but I have them come up one at a
time, like this. In this case, I think I had like 5 or 6 of these in the
example, so I would read these out, and I would take as long as I
wanted to read them. I may even go off on an adlib tangent on
something. That’s fine, because, again, you have nothing to lose here.
Now, you’re going to repeat the reminder slides. Again, you’re just
going to repeat this. Copy/paste. “Along with your Quick Start Guide,
you’ll also be receiving the complete new edition of The Every Other
Day Diet.” It’s the same exact thing. You just repeat it. It does not
matter that you’re repeating it. You don’t even need to reword it.
Same exact thing that we just went over in the last section. Same
exact thing.
Now, you’re going to repeat the CTA slide. Again, same thing, used
prior to this section. You want to use the one that you used right prior
to this section. You want to use the one that you use prior to this
section, so it’s just cut-and-paste.
Here’s an example. “Click the orange rectangular button below, and
let’s get you started now.” That’s the call to action we did right before
this started.
So, this is the end. We’ve just repeated everything, and this is the end.
And at this point, we just leave that on the screen, and that is the last
thing that your viewer will see.
Now, as an optional include, you can have a slide after this, of
testimonials. We do have that in the mind map. I actually like this. I
really do, because this is very hypnotic. It can go on and on and on.
But a lot of people just don’t have like 50 to 100 testimonials. So, what
I’ve done is I’ve just put in like 2 or 3 examples, to show you how it
would go.
I would show a slide this fast. And onward, and so forth. I just went
back and forth on these 2 slides, etc., etc. And you could show 50 of
them. It does not matter, because you’ve got the order button
underneath it.
And then, you’ll close with a final CTA, and you can leave this on the
screen as long as you want.

3
So, those last 2 parts were completely optional. I love them. I highly
suggest, if you have the testimonials, that you use them. You can use
pictures, head shot, testimonials. I just use text, to show you how
simple this could be. This is the last screen we’ll see.
This concludes the grand offer and your video sales letter.
